title: Fluffy Briny Filipino Shredded Meat Pancit-Bihon
description: A nostalgic Filipino dish featuring shredded meat, char-grill-yang flavors, and a briny, fluffy texture, perfect for a low-carb friendly lunch or dinner.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the shredded pork and cook until browned, about 3 minutes.
Add the char siu sauce, fish sauce, grated ginger, garlic, salt, black pepper, and sugar. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the Shrimp paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the Pancit Bihon noodles and cook until they start to brown, about 2-3 minutes.
Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the noodles are cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir in the green onions and scallions. Cook for 1 minute, or until the onions are wilted.
Transfer the Pancit Bihon to a serving platter and drizzle with Kalamansi juice.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Garnish with additional green onions and scallions if desired.
